title: NavigationProvider, ScreenEntry types, and screen registry
description:
Implement the NavigationProvider React context that manages the stack-based navigation model. This includes:

1. ScreenEntry interface: { id, screen: ScreenName, params: Record<string, string>, breadcrumb: string }
2. ScreenName enum: All screen identifiers from features.ts (Dashboard, RepoList, RepoOverview, Issues, IssueDetail, Landings, LandingDetail, DiffView, Workspaces, Workflows, Search, Notifications, Agents, Settings, Organizations, Sync, Wiki, plus detail/create/edit variants)
3. NavigationContext: stack, currentScreen, push(), pop(), replace(), reset() (aka goTo()), canGoBack, repoContext, orgContext
4. Screen registry map: Associates each ScreenName with { component, requiresRepo, requiresOrg, breadcrumbLabel }. Use lazy/placeholder components for all screens.
5. Stack constraints: Max depth 32, push-on-duplicate prevention, scroll position cache for back-navigation
6. useNavigation() hook: Provides NavigationContext to all descendants

Files: apps/tui/src/providers/NavigationProvider.tsx, apps/tui/src/router/types.ts, apps/tui/src/router/registry.ts, apps/tui/src/router/ScreenRouter.tsx, apps/tui/src/screens/PlaceholderScreen.tsx
acceptanceCriteria: NavigationProvider exposes push, pop, replace, reset via useNavigation() hook
ScreenEntry stores id, screen, params, breadcrumb
Max stack depth 32 enforced — push beyond 32 is a no-op
Duplicate push (same screen+params at top of stack) is silently dropped
pop() on depth-1 stack signals quit to the app
Screen registry maps all ScreenName values to placeholder components
repoContext derived from the deepest repo-scoped screen in the stack
ScreenRouter renders the component from registry for the top-of-stack entry

title: KeybindingProvider with layered priority dispatch
description:
Implement the KeybindingProvider that manages the layered keyboard input system. Includes:

1. KeybindingScope: { id, priority, bindings: Map<string, KeyHandler>, active }
2. Priority layers: Text input (P1) > Modal/Overlay (P2) > Go-to mode (P3) > Screen-specific (P4) > Global (P5)
3. KeyHandler: { key, description, group, handler, when? }
4. useScreenKeybindings(bindings): Push scope on mount, pop on unmount
5. useGlobalKeybindings(): Register always-active global bindings (q, Esc, Ctrl+C, ?, :, g)
6. Single useKeyboard() at provider level captures all input, walks priority stack top-down
7. Text input detection: focused <input>/<textarea> captures printable keys. Only Esc, Ctrl+C, Ctrl+S propagate.
8. useStatusBarHints(): Context hook for screens to register keybinding hints for status bar

Files: apps/tui/src/providers/KeybindingProvider.tsx, apps/tui/src/hooks/useScreenKeybindings.ts, apps/tui/src/hooks/useGlobalKeybindings.ts, apps/tui/src/hooks/useStatusBarHints.ts
acceptanceCriteria: KeybindingProvider captures all keyboard input via useKeyboard()
Scopes dispatched in priority order — higher priority consumes first
Text input focus suppresses printable keys from reaching navigation handlers
useScreenKeybindings pushes scope on mount, pops on unmount
useStatusBarHints allows screens to register hint arrays that update on screen change
Global keybindings (q, Esc, Ctrl+C) always registered at lowest priority
Conditional bindings (when?) evaluated before dispatch

title: Responsive layout hooks: useBreakpoint, useResponsiveValue, useLayout
description:
Implement the responsive layout detection system.

1. Breakpoint type: 'minimum' | 'standard' | 'large' (null for below-minimum)
2. getBreakpoint(cols, rows): <80||<24 → null, <120||<40 → minimum, <200||<60 → standard, else large
3. useBreakpoint(): Returns current breakpoint using useTerminalDimensions()
4. useResponsiveValue({ minimum, standard, large }): Returns value for current breakpoint
5. useLayout(): Composite hook returning { width, height, breakpoint, contentHeight, sidebarVisible, sidebarWidth, modalWidth, modalHeight }
6. Sidebar state: { userPreference: boolean|null, autoOverride: boolean } — tracks manual toggle separately from auto-collapse

Files: apps/tui/src/hooks/useBreakpoint.ts, apps/tui/src/hooks/useResponsiveValue.ts, apps/tui/src/hooks/useLayout.ts, apps/tui/src/hooks/useSidebarState.ts
acceptanceCriteria: useBreakpoint returns null when either dimension is below minimum (80x24)
useBreakpoint returns 'minimum' for 80x24 to 119x39
useBreakpoint returns 'standard' for 120x40 to 199x59
useBreakpoint returns 'large' for 200x60 and above
useResponsiveValue returns correct value for current breakpoint
useLayout computes contentHeight as height - 2
Sidebar state tracks user preference separately from auto-collapse
All hooks update synchronously on resize via useOnResize

title: OverlayManager context for mutual exclusion of modals
description:
Implement an OverlayManager React context ensuring only one overlay is visible at any time.

1. OverlayState: null | 'help' | 'command-palette' | 'confirm'
2. useOverlay(): Returns { activeOverlay, openOverlay(type), closeOverlay(), isOpen(type) }
3. openOverlay(type): If different overlay open, close first then open new. If same type, close (toggle).
4. OverlayLayer component: Renders active overlay as absolutely-positioned box with zIndex
5. Focus trapping: Integrates with KeybindingProvider priority layers

Files: apps/tui/src/providers/OverlayManager.tsx, apps/tui/src/components/OverlayLayer.tsx
acceptanceCriteria: Only one overlay visible at any time
Opening overlay when another is open closes the first
Toggle behavior: opening same overlay type closes it
OverlayLayer renders absolutely positioned content with zIndex
closeOverlay restores focus to underlying screen
Esc in any overlay triggers closeOverlay

title: GoToMode hook and state machine
description:
Implement the go-to mode state machine as a reusable hook.

1. useGoToMode(): Returns { active, activate(), cancel(), destinationHints: GoToHint[], error: string|null }
2. State machine: Inactive → press g → Active (1500ms timer) → second key resolves/cancels → Inactive
3. GoToHint[]: Static array of { key, destination, requiresRepo }
4. Timeout: 1500ms from activation, cleared on any second key or cancel
5. Timer cleanup: useEffect cleanup clears timeout on unmount
6. Error state: 'No repository in context' displayed for 2000ms then auto-clears

Files: apps/tui/src/hooks/useGoToMode.ts
acceptanceCriteria: useGoToMode returns active boolean reflecting current state
activate() sets active=true and starts 1500ms timer
cancel() sets active=false and clears timer
Timeout expiry sets active=false automatically
destinationHints returns all 11 go-to targets with requiresRepo flags
Error state holds message for 2000ms then clears
Timer cleaned up on component unmount

title: E2E test infrastructure: helpers.ts and app-shell test scaffold
description:
Create the foundational E2E test infrastructure for TUI testing using @microsoft/tui-test.

1. Test helper: e2e/tui/helpers.ts implementing TUITestInstance with sendKeys(), sendText(), waitForText(), waitForNoText(), snapshot(), getLine(), resize(), terminate(), rows, cols
2. launchTUI(): Spawns TUI process with configurable terminal dimensions, env vars, and CLI args
3. Test scaffold: e2e/tui/app-shell.test.ts with describe blocks for each feature in this group
4. Shared fixtures: Test env with CODEPLANE_TOKEN, test server URL, standard terminal sizes

Files: e2e/tui/helpers.ts, e2e/tui/app-shell.test.ts (scaffold)
acceptanceCriteria: launchTUI() spawns TUI process and returns TUITestInstance
sendKeys() sends keypress events to the terminal
waitForText() blocks until text appears in terminal output
snapshot() captures full terminal buffer as string
getLine() returns specific terminal row content
resize() changes terminal dimensions and triggers SIGWINCH
terminate() cleanly shuts down the TUI process
app-shell.test.ts has describe blocks for all 8 features in this group

title: AppShell component with three-zone layout
description:
Implement the AppShell root layout component providing three-zone structure (header, content, status bar) plus overlay layer.

1. AppShell: <box flexDirection='column' width='100%' height={height}> containing HeaderBar (1 row), content (flexGrow=1), StatusBar (1 row), OverlayLayer (absolute)
2. Terminal-too-small guard: When useBreakpoint() returns null, render centered message. Only Ctrl+C active.
3. Content area: Renders ScreenRouter showing top-of-stack screen
4. Integration: AppShell is innermost element in provider stack

Files: apps/tui/src/components/AppShell.tsx
acceptanceCriteria: AppShell renders header bar, content area, and status bar in vertical flex layout
Header bar occupies exactly 1 row at top
Status bar occupies exactly 1 row at bottom
Content area fills remaining height with flexGrow=1
OverlayLayer renders above content with absolute positioning
Terminal-too-small message shown when breakpoint is null
Only Ctrl+C active when terminal is too small

title: Deep-link argument parser and stack builder
description:
Implement CLI argument parser for deep-link launch flags and stack pre-population logic.

1. parseDeepLinkArgs(argv): Parses --screen, --repo, --org from process argv
2. validateDeepLinkArgs(args): Validates screen against allowlist (case-insensitive), repo against regex, org against regex. Returns { valid, error?, normalizedScreen? }
3. buildInitialStack(validatedArgs): Returns ScreenEntry[] following spec's stack pre-population rules
4. Supported screens: dashboard, repos, issues, landings, workspaces, workflows, search, notifications, agents, settings, orgs, sync, wiki
5. Repo-required: issues, landings, workflows, wiki
6. Error message truncation: 32/64/32 chars, control character stripping

Files: apps/tui/src/deep-link/parser.ts, apps/tui/src/deep-link/validator.ts, apps/tui/src/deep-link/stack-builder.ts
acceptanceCriteria: parseDeepLinkArgs correctly extracts --screen, --repo, --org from argv
validateDeepLinkArgs rejects unknown screen values with descriptive error
validateDeepLinkArgs rejects repo values not matching OWNER/REPO pattern
Case-insensitive screen comparison (Issues → issues)
buildInitialStack returns [Dashboard] for no args
buildInitialStack returns [Dashboard, Repo, Issues] for --screen issues --repo owner/repo
Error messages truncate long values and strip control characters

title: TUI_SCREEN_ROUTER — Stack-based screen navigation
description:
Implement the complete stack-based screen router. Build on NavigationProvider and KeybindingProvider.

1. Router-level keybindings: q (pop/quit), Esc (close overlay → pop), Ctrl+C (quit), ?, :, g
2. Screen transitions under 50ms. Previous screen unmounted after new screen first render.
3. Back-navigation state restore: scroll position and focused element cached per entry
4. Loading states: centered spinner while data hooks return loading
5. Edge cases: rapid q, q during loading, duplicate push, max depth 32
6. Auth gating: unauthenticated → auth error screen. 401 mid-session → auth expired screen.

E2E tests: router-initial-render, q-pops, q-quits-root, escape-closes-overlay, ctrl-c-quits, enter-pushes, q-restores-scroll, rapid-q, double-enter, loading-state, auth-error, error-boundary

Files: apps/tui/src/router/ScreenRouter.tsx, apps/tui/src/hooks/useGlobalKeybindings.ts, apps/tui/src/components/LoadingScreen.tsx, apps/tui/src/components/AuthErrorScreen.tsx
acceptanceCriteria: Pushing a screen adds it to stack and renders its content
Popping removes top screen and renders previous
Root screen pop signals TUI quit
q/Esc/Ctrl+C global keybindings work on every screen
Text input focus suppresses q/g/?/: navigation keys
Screen transitions complete under 50ms
Loading spinner shown while screen data loads
Back-navigation restores scroll position and focus
Max stack depth 32 enforced
Duplicate push is no-op
Auth error screen when unauthenticated
All router E2E tests pass

title: TUI_HEADER_BAR — Persistent header with breadcrumbs, repo context, badges
description:
Implement the header bar component occupying top row of every screen.

1. Left zone: Breadcrumb segments from nav stack separated by › (U+203A). Current in primary, parents in muted. Truncation from left with …. Segment max 24 chars, repo max 30 chars.
2. Center zone: owner/repo when in repo scope. Hidden at <120 cols.
3. Right zone: Connection indicator (green ● / red ○) from SSE. Notification badge [N] in warning when >0, hidden at 0, capped [99+]. Real-time SSE updates.

Responsive: 80-99 truncated+hidden center, 100-119 truncate if needed, 120+ full, 200+ comfortable spacing.

E2E tests: header renders at all sizes, breadcrumb depth, truncation, notification badge states, connection indicator, keyboard updates, resize tests

Files: apps/tui/src/components/HeaderBar.tsx, apps/tui/src/utils/breadcrumb.ts
acceptanceCriteria: Header bar renders as 1-row box at top of every screen
Breadcrumb segments separated by › with correct coloring
Current segment primary color, parents muted
Truncation from left with … when exceeding width
Center zone shows repo context at ≥120 cols, hidden at <120
Connection indicator shows ● green connected, ○ red disconnected
Notification badge [N] warning color when >0, hidden at 0, capped [99+]
Badge updates in real-time via SSE
Header remains visible during modal overlays
All header bar E2E tests pass

title: TUI_STATUS_BAR — Persistent footer with hints, sync, notifications
description:
Implement status bar pinned to bottom row. Three sections:

1. Left: Context-sensitive key:action hints. Bold keys, muted actions. Truncated with …. Go-to mode replaces with destination hints. 4/6/all hints per breakpoint.
2. Center: Sync status — connected (green ●), syncing (braille spinner), conflict (▲ count), disconnected (red ●). Icon-only at <120 cols.
3. Right: ◆ + count (primary when >0, muted when 0), 2s bold flash on increase. ?:help always rightmost. Count capped 99+.

Background surface color. Full width. 1 row.

E2E tests: SNAP-SB, KEY-SB, RESIZE-SB, RT-SB, EDGE-SB test series

Files: apps/tui/src/components/StatusBar.tsx, apps/tui/src/components/SyncStatusIndicator.tsx, apps/tui/src/components/NotificationBadge.tsx
acceptanceCriteria: Status bar renders as 1-row box with surface background at bottom
Left section shows context-sensitive keybinding hints
Hints update within one render frame on screen change
During go-to mode, left section shows destination hints
Center shows sync status with correct icon/color per state
Braille spinner animates via useTimeline()
At <120 cols sync collapses to icon-only
Right section shows ◆+count when >0, muted when 0
2-second bold flash on new notification
?:help always visible rightmost
All status bar E2E tests pass

title: TUI_RESPONSIVE_LAYOUT — Breakpoint system, sidebar toggle, terminal-too-small
description:
Integrate responsive layout hooks into the full application.

1. Breakpoint-driven adaptations in HeaderBar, StatusBar, AppShell, ScreenRouter
2. Terminal-too-small screen with only Ctrl+C active
3. Sidebar toggle (Ctrl+B) with auto-collapse at minimum and user preference tracking
4. Resize handling: synchronous, no flicker, scroll preserved, stack intact, modals re-center
5. Content adaptation: list columns per breakpoint, diff locks unified at minimum, modal 90%/60%/50%

E2E tests: RESPONSIVE_SNAPSHOT_01-17, KEY_01-09, RESIZE_01-14, INTEGRATION_01-08

Files: Enhance AppShell.tsx, add SidebarLayout.tsx, enhance hooks
acceptanceCriteria: Three breakpoints correctly detected
Below 80x24 shows terminal-too-small with only Ctrl+C
Resize from too-small restores full layout with stack preserved
Ctrl+B toggles sidebar on sidebar-having screens
Sidebar starts hidden at minimum, visible at standard/large
Sidebar auto-hides on resize to minimum, restores on resize back
User manual hide preserved across breakpoint changes
Synchronous re-layout with no flicker
Modal overlays resize per breakpoint
All responsive E2E tests pass

title: TUI_COMMAND_PALETTE — Fuzzy search modal overlay
description:
Implement command palette modal activated by :.

1. Activation: : opens palette. Blocked in text inputs. Immediate focus on search input.
2. Fuzzy search from @codeplane/ui-core. Ranking: prefix > contiguous > non-contiguous. Case-insensitive.
3. Command registry: PaletteCommand with id, name, description, category, keybinding, action, contextRequirements, featureFlag.
4. Context filtering: repo-scoped hidden when no repo. Feature-flagged hidden.
5. j/k/Up/Down navigation with wrap-around. Enter executes. Highlighted row.
6. Centered overlay, responsive 90%/60%/50%. Footer hints.
7. Esc/Ctrl+C dismisses. Input cleared between invocations. Focus trapped.

E2E tests: rendering, keyboard nav, fuzzy search, context filtering, responsive, edge cases

Files: apps/tui/src/components/CommandPalette.tsx, apps/tui/src/commands/registry.ts, apps/tui/src/commands/types.ts
acceptanceCriteria: : key opens command palette as centered modal
: passes colon to text input when focused
Fuzzy search filters in real-time with correct ranking
All go-to targets available as Navigate commands
Repo-scoped commands hidden without repo context
j/k navigates results with wrap-around
Enter executes and closes, Esc dismisses
Input cleared each open
Focus trapped
Responsive sizing per breakpoint
Opens <50ms, filters <16ms
All command palette E2E tests pass

title: TUI_HELP_OVERLAY — Keybinding reference modal
description:
Implement help overlay showing all keybindings for current context.

1. Toggle: ? opens/closes. Esc closes.
2. Groups: Global, Go To, screen-specific. Headings in primary+bold. Two-column: keys warning, descriptions muted.
3. Global always shown. Go-to group with all 11 destinations. Screen-specific via HelpOverlayContext.
4. Scrolling: j/k/Up/Down, G, gg, Ctrl+D/U. Scroll indicator.
5. Focus trapping. : suppressed while open.
6. Mutual exclusion with command palette.
7. Responsive 90%/60%. No data dependency.

E2E tests: rendering, keyboard (toggle, scroll), responsive, context changes, mutual exclusion

Files: apps/tui/src/components/HelpOverlay.tsx, apps/tui/src/providers/HelpOverlayContext.tsx
acceptanceCriteria: ? toggles help overlay
Global keybinding group always shown
Go To group shows all 11 destinations
Screen-specific keybindings dynamically included
Groups have labeled headings
Two-column layout with correct colors
Scrollable with j/k/G/gg/Ctrl+D/U
Focus trapped, : suppressed
Mutual exclusion with command palette
Responsive sizing
All help overlay E2E tests pass

title: TUI_DEEP_LINK_LAUNCH — CLI argument-driven screen navigation
description:
Integrate deep-link parser into TUI bootstrap.

1. Parse --screen/--repo/--org before React mount. Pass to NavigationProvider for initial stack.
2. Stack pre-population per spec rules.
3. Validation failure → Dashboard + 5s transient status bar error.
4. Auth completes before screen navigation (no Dashboard flash).
5. Back-navigation through pre-populated stack.
6. Responsive breadcrumb truncation. Error message truncation.
7. Input sanitization: control chars stripped, values truncated.

E2E tests: deep-link snapshot, keyboard, responsive, integration tests

Files: Enhance index.tsx, NavigationProvider.tsx, add TransientError.tsx
acceptanceCriteria: --screen launches to specified screen
--repo sets repository context
Stack pre-populated with intermediate screens
q walks back through pre-populated stack
Unknown screen → Dashboard + 5s error
Missing repo → Dashboard + 5s error
Invalid format → Dashboard + 5s error
Errors auto-clear after 5 seconds
Auth loading before deep-linked screen
All deep-link E2E tests pass

title: TUI_GOTO_KEYBINDINGS — Two-key chord navigation
description:
Implement full go-to keybinding system on useGoToMode hook.

1. g activates mode. Suppressed during input/overlays/too-small.
2. All 11 destinations wired to NavigationContext.reset().
3. Stack replacement: g+d → [Dashboard], g+i → [Dashboard, Repo, Issues].
4. Context check: i/l/f/k need repo. No repo → 2s error.
5. Cancellation: Esc, q+pop, Ctrl+C, invalid key, 1500ms timeout.
6. Status bar: destination hints during mode, truncated at small widths.
7. Help overlay: Go To group with 11 entries.
8. Edge cases: g+g cancels, rapid toggle, resize during mode.

E2E tests: 52 tests — snapshot (1-10), keyboard (11-39), responsive (41-47), integration (48-52)

Files: Enhance useGoToMode.ts, add useGoToKeybindings.ts, enhance status bar and help overlay
acceptanceCriteria: g activates go-to mode with status bar hints
All 11 destinations navigate correctly
Context-free destinations work without repo
Repo-required destinations check context
Missing repo → 2s error
Stack replaced (reset not push)
1500ms timeout cancels
Esc cancels without pop, q cancels and pops
Suppressed during input/overlays/too-small
Go To group in help overlay
All go-to E2E tests pass
